title: DAST and SAST Finding Density Before Payment Service Releases

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 6.3.2 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 6.3.2 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.38: DAST and SAST Finding Density Before Payment Service Releases.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.38' running on index=devsecops sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan") ‚Äî app_name, build_id, severity, status, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=devsecops sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan") earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_payment_apps.csv app_name OUTPUT pci_app
| where pci_app="true"
| where severity IN ("High","Very High","Critical")
| where match(status,"(?i)open|new)")
| stats count by app_name, build_id, scan_type
| where count>0
| sort - count
description: Tracks high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment APIs so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data.
value: Tracks high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment APIs so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data.
implementation: (1) Map scanner project names to internal `app_name`. (2) Gate release on `count=0` for Critical if policy mandates. (3) Correlate with UC-22.11.37 for same `build_id`. (4) Quarterly trending for PCI interview.
visualization: Stacked bar (open highs by app), table (build_id), line (count over time).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We track high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom `sourcetype="veracode:scan"` / `checkmarx:scan"` HEC normalizers.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=devsecops` `sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan")` ‚Äî `app_name`, `build_id`, `severity`, `status`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Map scanner project names to internal `app_name`. (2) Gate release on `count=0` for Critical if policy mandates. (3) Correlate with UC-22.11.37 for same `build_id`. (4) Quarterly trending for PCI interview.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devsecops sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan") earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_payment_apps.csv app_name OUTPUT pci_app
| where pci_app="true"
| where severity IN ("High","Very High","Critical")
| where match(status,"(?i)open|new)")
| stats count by app_name, build_id, scan_type
| where count>0
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**DAST and SAST Finding Density Before Payment Service Releases (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 6.3.2)** ‚Äî Tracks high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment APIs so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=devsecops` `sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan")` ‚Äî `app_name`, `build_id`, `severity`, `status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom `sourcetype="veracode:scan"` / `checkmarx:scan"` HEC normalizers. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devsecops.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=devsecops, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where pci_app="true"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where severity IN ("High","Very High","Critical")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(status,"(?i)open|new)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_name, build_id, scan_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count>0` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**DAST and SAST Finding Density Before Payment Service Releases (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 6.3.2)** ‚Äî Tracks high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment APIs so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=devsecops` `sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan")` ‚Äî `app_name`, `build_id`, `severity`, `status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom `sourcetype="veracode:scan"` / `checkmarx:scan"` HEC normalizers. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (open highs by app), table (build_id), line (count over time).
